sect1.title: BIND Utilities-&bind-version;
sect1.sect2:
Introduction to BIND Utilities
BIND Utilities is not a separate package, it is a collection of the client side programs that are included with . The BIND package includes the client side programs nslookup, dig and host. If you install BIND server, these programs will be installed automatically. This section is for those users who don't need the complete BIND server, but need these client side applications.
&lfs130_checked;
Package Information
Download (HTTP):
Download (FTP):
Download MD5 sum: &bind-md5sum;
Download size: &bind-size;
Estimated disk space required: &bind-utils-buildsize;
Estimated build time: &bind-utils-time;
BIND Utilities Dependencies
Required
and
Recommended
and
Optional
, , and

sect2.title: Installation of BIND Utilities
sect2.para: Install BIND Utilities by running the following commands:

screen.userinput: ./configure --prefix=/usr --sysconfdir=/etc && make -C lib/isc && make -C lib/dns && make -C lib/ns && make -C lib/isccfg && make -C lib/isccc && make -C bin/dig && make -C bin/nsupdate && make -C bin/rndc && make -C doc

sect2.para: This portion of the package does not come with a test suite.

screen.userinput: make -C lib/isc install && make -C lib/dns install && make -C lib/ns install && make -C lib/isccfg install && make -C lib/isccc install && make -C bin/dig install && make -C bin/nsupdate install && make -C bin/rndc install && cp -v doc/man/{dig.1,host.1,nslookup.1,nsupdate.1} /usr/share/man/man1 && cp -v doc/man/rndc.8 /usr/share/man/man8

sect2.title: Command Explanations
sect2.para: --sysconfdir=/etc: Use this option to ensure installed packages look in /etc for configuration files.
--disable-doh: Use this option if you have not installed and you don't need DNS over HTTPS support.
make -C lib/...: These commands build the libraries that are needed for the client programs.
make -C bin/...: This command builds the client programs.
make -C doc: This command builds the manual pages if the optional Python module is installed.
Use cp -v doc/man/... /usr/share/man/man... to install the manual pages if they have been built.

sect2.title: Contents

seglistitem.seg: dig, host, nslookup, nsupdate, and rndc
libdns.so, libisc.so, libisccc.so, libisccfg.so, and libns.so

sect2.para: See the program descriptions in the section.
